era: 1990-1997 core. Grunge Punk. E=7 -- fast, raw, abrasive, underground. Seattle. Empty Records.

theory_nodes.concept: Empty Records enters the engine at 229. Seattle's most deliberately underground label -- anti-commercial, noise-forward, below the Sub Pop and Amphetamine Reptile tiers.
Funny car as band metaphor: maximum performance, minimum distance, burns methanol (alcohol). The band name is a drag racing reference and a philosophical statement about how to operate.
theory_nodes.description: Drag racing metaphor as music philosophy.

theory_nodes.concept: Grunge-punk as the punk substrate of grunge. Wipers (Portland), Husker Du, Replacements as direct antecedents. Alcohol Funnycar inherits the fast-tempo, distorted-guitar, lo-fi production tradition.
theory_nodes.description: Punk roots of the Seattle sound.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 continues. Amphetamine Reptile to Empty Records. Underground tier sequence.
E drops to 6. Back to Sub Pop. Fremont. The energy eases after the underground peak.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7+E=6=E=13. Above equilibrium. Largest buteo meets loudest underground band. Both are outsized in their respective domains.
